title: galium mollugo var. cinereum
intro: Galium mollugo var. cinereum, also known as the pale bedstraw or cinereous bedstraw, is a plant species in the bedstraw family (Rubiaceae) that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat urinary tract issues, as well as to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gout. The health benefits of Galium mollugo var. cinereum include its potential to help reduce blood pressure, improve kidney function, and provide relief from pain and swelling. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
